title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Demott Cluttercuck and his superior discuss the suspicious circumstances surrounding the poisoning of Heather Badcock. They note that the crime occurred during an afternoon event where a prescription drug was administered via a cocktail, a method they consider risky given the presence of twenty to thirty people. While the local detective, Cornish, observed that Heather's husband, Arthur Badcock, appeared tense, Cluttercuck concluded that the couple seemed loyal. Cluttercuck decides to take Tidler, a movie enthusiast, to investigate the case.

Detective Moore (also referred to as Dermot Clark) visits Miss Marple to inform her that Scotland Yard has taken over the murder investigation. Miss Marple reflects on Heather's character, noting that while she was a good housekeeper, she was not particularly considerate, which may have left her husband lonely. Miss Marple suggests that the detectives visit Lady Bunty, who lives in a cottage at Gossington Manor and attended the party. She advises them to mention "Charlotte's Daughters" to Lady Bunty to prompt relevant information.

Later, Cluttercuck meets Mrs. Bantry. She observes that although there is no official evidence regarding how the poison was administered, the community treats the incident as a murder. However, she finds it difficult to imagine a motive; while she describes Heather as an unpleasant, overly emotional, and over-the-top woman, she does not believe anyone would have wanted to kill her.
